title: What large language models know and forget
notes: Explained and contrasted via the closed-book baseline; there is no real LLM parameter store.

title: What large language models know and forget
notes: Demonstrated by the inability of closed-book mode to answer source-sensitive corpus questions.

title: What large language models know and forget
concept: Need for provenance on local facts
notes: Grounded answers always attach evidence-backed citations.

title: Why retrieval is needed
concept: Retrieval as an explicit evidence path
notes: Retrieval is the default path for grounded answers.

title: Why retrieval is needed
notes: Freshness and role-aware disclosure are present; no external private DB.

title: Why retrieval is needed
notes: The system abstains or asks for clarification instead of inventing support.

title: Foundations of information retrieval
notes: The code uses explicit document, chunk, query, hit, and score objects.

title: Foundations of information retrieval
notes: A small BM25 index is built from chunk tokens.

title: Foundations of information retrieval
notes: Recall@k, Precision@k, MRR, and nDCG are included.

title: The core architecture of basic RAG
concept: Retriever, reranker, generator, knowledge base boundaries
notes: Modules are intentionally separated along the book's architectural boundaries.

title: The core architecture of basic RAG
notes: The `answer` command is the fixed retrieve->rerank->pack->synthesize path.

title: The core architecture of basic RAG
notes: Offline staging and online live snapshots are separate.

title: A minimal end-to-end RAG pipeline
notes: Ingest/index/publish are separate from retrieve/answer/agent.

title: A minimal end-to-end RAG pipeline
notes: Chunks are stored as JSONL and indexed separately.

title: A minimal end-to-end RAG pipeline
notes: The system records traces and abstains when support is weak.

title: Documents, corpora, and chunking
notes: Markdown front matter becomes document metadata.

title: Documents, corpora, and chunking
notes: Headings and markdown tables become chunk boundaries and row chunks.

title: Documents, corpora, and chunking
notes: Low-OCR and malicious documents are quarantined; PDF OCR is simulated.

title: Embeddings and vector representations
notes: Hashed vectors and cosine similarity drive dense retrieval.

title: Embeddings and vector representations
concept: Dual-encoder style offline chunk vectors / online query vectors
notes: The hash vectorizer plays the role of a stand-in encoder.

title: Embeddings and vector representations
notes: Discussed honestly; not benchmarked against alternate embedding models.

title: Indexing and approximate nearest neighbor search
notes: Random-hyperplane LSH provides an approximate dense candidate set.

title: Indexing and approximate nearest neighbor search
notes: ANN can be toggled with `--no-ann`; tables and budgets are documented rather than learned.

title: Indexing and approximate nearest neighbor search
notes: The repository explains them but implements a simpler LSH alternative.

title: Query understanding and first-pass retrieval
notes: Identifiers such as SB-118 and V14 are preserved and boosted.

title: Query understanding and first-pass retrieval
concept: Ambiguity detection and clarification before blind retrieval
notes: Ambiguous Phoenix and under-scoped policy queries ask for clarification.

title: Query understanding and first-pass retrieval
notes: Region and date filters exist; full source-system mismatch simulation is limited.

title: Reranking and relevance refinement
notes: Heuristic reranking refines the first-pass candidate set.

title: Reranking and relevance refinement
notes: Authority, product, region, quality, trust, and superseded status influence ranking.

title: Reranking and relevance refinement
notes: Duplicate docs and same-document redundancy are downweighted heuristically.

title: Prompt assembly and context packing
notes: PackedContext respects a token budget and keeps the highest-value spans.

title: Prompt assembly and context packing
notes: Sentence scoring chooses the most query-relevant evidence spans.

title: Prompt assembly and context packing
notes: Conflicts are surfaced as notes rather than silently erased.

title: Grounded generation and answer synthesis
notes: The generator is explicitly evidence-first and extractive.

title: Grounded generation and answer synthesis
notes: Unsupported or under-scoped questions produce abstention or clarification.

title: Grounded generation and answer synthesis
notes: Claims are checked against cited evidence spans.

title: Evaluating basic RAG systems
notes: Recall@k, Precision@k, MRR, and nDCG are computed.

title: Evaluating basic RAG systems
notes: Groundedness and expected-fragment hit rate are reported.

title: Evaluating basic RAG systems
concept: Dirty corpora and false negatives in evaluation
notes: The benchmark corpus includes noisy and quarantined inputs, but label incompleteness is only lightly simulated.

title: Sparse, dense, and hybrid retrieval
notes: BM25-style lexical ranking is available.

title: Sparse, dense, and hybrid retrieval
notes: Hashed vector search with cosine similarity is available.

title: Sparse, dense, and hybrid retrieval
notes: Hybrid retrieval uses reciprocal rank fusion.

title: Metadata filtering and structured retrieval
notes: Region, product, disclosure, and date filters constrain eligibility.

title: Metadata filtering and structured retrieval
notes: Retrieval happens only over chunks the user may see.

title: Metadata filtering and structured retrieval
notes: Service-center lookup is done through a structured JSON-backed tool.

title: Query rewriting, expansion, and decomposition
notes: Alias rules such as rollback -> credential recovery are applied.

title: Query rewriting, expansion, and decomposition
notes: Subqueries are extracted heuristically; full aggregation logic is modest.

title: Query rewriting, expansion, and decomposition
concept: Ask the user instead of guessing missing scope
notes: Ambiguous policy and Phoenix queries ask rather than hallucinating scope.

title: Multi-hop retrieval and iterative search
notes: AgentState tracks evidence, blockers, and action history across steps.

title: Multi-hop retrieval and iterative search
notes: Explicit references like HMX-44 and RB-X12-STAGING-ROTATE trigger follow-up search.

title: Multi-hop retrieval and iterative search
concept: Stopping criteria and bounded search budgets
notes: The controller stops after sufficiency or budget exhaustion.

title: Long-context RAG and context compression
notes: Context selection remains budgeted even though the demo corpus is small.

title: Long-context RAG and context compression
notes: Sentence selection compresses long chunks.

title: Long-context RAG and context compression
notes: The repository avoids abstractive compression because it would require a more capable model.

title: Knowledge base freshness and incremental updates
notes: Snapshots are built, published, and listed explicitly.

title: Knowledge base freshness and incremental updates
notes: The system rebuilds from source roots; it does not stream source events.

title: Knowledge base freshness and incremental updates
notes: Superseded documents are penalized and active+superseded conflicts are surfaced.

title: Personalization, session memory, and adaptive context
concept: Session memory and scoped memory writes
notes: The controller can persist task-scoped memory entries in session files.

title: Personalization, session memory, and adaptive context
concept: Adaptive retrieval from user profile and session state
notes: User region and remembered product/region can bias retrieval.

title: Personalization, session memory, and adaptive context
notes: Conflicting product memory is pruned; more advanced contamination control is documented only.

title: What makes a RAG system agentic
notes: AgentState plus a bounded action loop make the system genuinely stateful.

title: What makes a RAG system agentic
notes: The `answer` and `agent` commands intentionally separate fixed workflow from adaptive control.

title: What makes a RAG system agentic
notes: Traces record retrieval, tool calls, clarification, and stop decisions.

title: Tools, APIs, and function calling
notes: Each tool declares required fields, enums, and read-only vs side-effecting status.

title: Tools, APIs, and function calling
notes: Tool calls return normalized payloads instead of raw text.

title: Tools, APIs, and function calling
notes: A side-effecting tool schema exists but is blocked unless governance approves it.

title: Planning and task decomposition
notes: The planner chooses retrieval, tool, clarification, or follow-up patterns from task type.

title: Planning and task decomposition
notes: Under-scoped tasks clarify before retrieval continues.

title: Planning and task decomposition
notes: The controller changes route or follows references after observing retrieved evidence.

title: Retrieval as an action inside agent loops
notes: Retrieval is one controller action among tool use, clarification, and synthesis.

title: Retrieval as an action inside agent loops
notes: Low-support cases escalate from cheap or sparse routes to hybrid ones.

title: Retrieval as an action inside agent loops
concept: Ask the user when scope is missing
notes: The controller distinguishes missing user intent from corpus-discoverable follow-ups.

title: Working memory, state, and control flow
notes: AgentState stores evidence, blockers, open questions, and scoped memory entries.

title: Working memory, state, and control flow
notes: The controller changes behavior when waiting for the user, following references, or synthesizing.

title: Working memory, state, and control flow
concept: Prompt is not the authoritative state
notes: State lives outside model prompts in JSON-serializable objects.

title: Routing across retrievers, tools, and models
notes: Exact identifiers favor sparse routes; harder tasks use hybrid routes.

title: Routing across retrievers, tools, and models
concept: Routing across structured tools vs text retrieval
notes: Structured requests route to record lookups first.

title: Routing across retrievers, tools, and models
notes: Fast vs careful synthesis profiles simulate model-family routing without external models.

title: Reflection, critique, and self-correction
notes: Weak evidence, missing scope, and conflicts trigger retries, clarification, or escalation.

title: Reflection, critique, and self-correction
notes: The controller distinguishes route changes from explicit user clarification.

title: Reflection, critique, and self-correction
notes: Search budgets and high-stakes escalation prevent endless retries.

title: Multi-agent RAG architectures
notes: A small specialized multi-agent path is included.

title: Multi-agent RAG architectures
notes: The verifier can mark claims unsupported or recommend escalation.

title: Multi-agent RAG architectures
notes: The repo includes one coordination pattern, but broader coordination trade-offs remain in docs.

title: Human-in-the-loop agent workflows
notes: The controller can stop and ask for missing region or policy family.

title: Human-in-the-loop agent workflows
notes: Role-based disclosure is enforced; richer approval workflows are documented.

title: Human-in-the-loop agent workflows
notes: Escalation text exists, but reviewer ticketing is simulated rather than integrated.

title: Groundedness, attribution, and citation
notes: Claims are checked against supporting evidence spans.

title: Groundedness, attribution, and citation
notes: Citation objects point back to chunk, doc, and section.

title: Groundedness, attribution, and citation
notes: Verification runs after the answer draft is formed.

title: Failure modes and hallucination in RAG
notes: The code surfaces stale, conflicting, and unsupported-evidence cases; a real generative hallucination model is out of scope.

title: Failure modes and hallucination in RAG
notes: Conflicting or superseded evidence is retained and annotated rather than silently blended.

title: Failure modes and hallucination in RAG
notes: Low-OCR and malicious documents are quarantined.

title: Security threats: prompt injection, retrieval poisoning, and data leakage
notes: Heuristics flag instruction-like text in retrieved documents.

title: Security threats: prompt injection, retrieval poisoning, and data leakage
notes: Low-trust content is quarantined or excluded from eligible chunks.

title: Security threats: prompt injection, retrieval poisoning, and data leakage
notes: Tool runtime validation plus role-based policy gates are present.

title: Evaluation of agentic RAG systems
notes: The benchmark reports retrieval, answer, clarification, and escalation-related metrics.

title: Evaluation of agentic RAG systems
notes: Clarification precision and recall are computed.

title: Evaluation of agentic RAG systems
notes: Escalation rate is measured; richer retry-gain analysis is documented.

title: Latency, caching, and cost optimization
notes: Trace spans make route, retrieval, and tool latency visible.

title: Latency, caching, and cost optimization
notes: A file cache exists and routing models cheap vs careful paths; integrated cache use is intentionally lightweight.

title: Latency, caching, and cost optimization
notes: The controller has bounded steps and route escalation thresholds.

title: Observability, tracing, and debugging
notes: Every agent run and many answer flows emit structured JSON traces.

title: Observability, tracing, and debugging
concept: Debugging workflows from symptom to root cause
notes: The docs show how to inspect ingest, retrieval, routing, and generation artifacts.

title: Observability, tracing, and debugging
concept: Lineage from serving back to snapshot artifacts
notes: Traces and live pointers connect requests to snapshot versions.

title: Governance, privacy, and compliance
notes: Roles determine allowed disclosures before retrieval.

title: Governance, privacy, and compliance
notes: Retention tags and auditable traces exist, but no external deletion workflow is integrated.

title: Governance, privacy, and compliance
concept: Purpose limitation and human approval gates
notes: Action approval is a toy predicate rather than a full enterprise policy engine.

title: Enterprise knowledge assistants and domain agents
concept: Domain grounding by role and workflow
notes: Support and distributor tasks use different routes, disclosures, and tools.

title: Enterprise knowledge assistants and domain agents
notes: Structured service-center and runbook tools simulate domain-specific integrations.

title: Enterprise knowledge assistants and domain agents
notes: Domain demos and role-aware behavior exist; real business KPIs are documented only.

title: Production architectures and deployment patterns
notes: Ingest/index/publish happen offline; retrieve/answer/agent happen on live snapshots.

title: Production architectures and deployment patterns
notes: Quarantine and publish are implemented; rollback is manual by selecting a previous snapshot.

title: Production architectures and deployment patterns
notes: Config values are manifest-backed; there is no external control plane.

title: Future directions in agentic knowledge systems
concept: Documented vs inferred vs speculative futures
notes: This chapter is intentionally narrative and not mapped to executable code.

title: Future directions in agentic knowledge systems
concept: Richer evidence structures and stronger controllers
notes: Discussed as future work, not implemented directly.

title: Future directions in agentic knowledge systems
concept: Stable principles that survive future model changes
notes: The repo closes by naming enduring design principles rather than adding more code.
